sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.73 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: Let${ F}$be any local formation of finite soluble groups containing all finite nilpotent groups. Prove that$H {{ F}}K=KH {{ F}}$for any two subnormal subgroups$H$and$K$of an arbitrary finite group$G$. { L.\,A.\,Shemetkov} This has been proved, even without the hypothesis of solubility of${ F}$\ (S.\,F.\,Ka\-mor\-ni\-kov, Dokl. Akad. Nauk BSSR, { 33}, no.\,5 (1989), 396--399 (Russian)).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
